name: Rosemary Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 1 cup fresh rosemary leaves (plus long sprigs for garnish, rinsed and drained well)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a clean 1-quart glass jar, combine the rosemary leaves, halved garlic cloves, and red-wine vinegar.
2. Cover the jar with its lid and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for at least 4 days to up to 2 weeks, depending on desired strength.
3. Strain the liquid into a glass pitcher, discarding the garlic and rosemary leaves.
4. Pour the strained vinegar into two clean 1/2-pint glass jars.
5. Add one sprig of rosemary and some reserved garlic from the straining step to each jar.
6. Seal the jars tightly with lids.
